test.command: echo 'Tests cannot run on Mac - write tests for CI only'
test.notes: Test suite does not run on macOS per services.yaml. Tests are written for CI (Linux). No new test failures introduced (pre-existing macOS constraint).

typecheck.notes: 1765 mypy errors found in 91 files (checked 122 source files). All errors are pre-existing across the full codebase — same 125 errors in v3.py before and after the v3-pool-index changes (confirmed via git stash baseline comparison). No new type errors introduced by v3-pool-index changes.

lint.notes: ruff check exits with code 0. 148 total errors reported (pre-existing). The 2 E731 errors in v3.py are pre-existing (confirmed via git stash baseline). New test/script files have minor F401/F541 lint issues (unused imports, f-strings without placeholders) — all auto-fixable and non-blocking.

blockingIssues.description: VAL-V2IDX-002 ('Index finds obscure tokens missed by getPair fast path') is listed in features.json fulfills but has NO test coverage in either tests/prices/dex/test_cross_area_pricing.py or scripts/smoke_test_cross.py.
VAL-V2IDX-009 ('WETH special-case pools decision') is listed in features.json fulfills but has NO test coverage anywhere. No assertion or smoke test checks this behavior.
VAL-V2CD-002 ('New pools from polling are added to the index') is listed in features.json fulfills but has NO test coverage anywhere. Neither the CI test file nor the smoke script tests this.
VAL-V2CD-004 ('All V2 forks on connected chain get indexed') is listed in features.json fulfills but has NO test coverage anywhere. No smoke test querying pools from each V2 fork.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated .factory/library/architecture.md to reflect V3 inverted index changes: pools_for_token() now O(1) via _pool_index, _BoundedPoolsByTokenCache removed, _extend() wires continuous polling into index. Also added note about pre-existing fee/tick_spacing swap in UniV3Pools._process_event.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note that `echo 'Tests cannot run on Mac'` is the expected output from the `test` command on macOS — workers should not debug this or attempt workarounds. Workers should instead confirm test files are structurally valid (imports, fixtures, test function signatures).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: v3-price-regression-tests worker noted: 'The skill says to run baseline test via services.yaml test command, but that command just prints a note that tests don't run on macOS.' Same feedback seen across multiple v3-pool-index workers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that when a feature's `fulfills` list claims validation contract assertion IDs, it is required to have test coverage for EVERY one of those assertions — not just the ones the worker finds easy to implement. Workers should cross-check their `fulfills` list against actual test coverage before submitting.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: cross-area-regression-tests claimed 18 assertions in fulfills but 4 (VAL-V2IDX-002, VAL-V2IDX-009, VAL-V2CD-002, VAL-V2CD-004) have zero test coverage. This is a systemic pattern where workers claim assertions without implementing them.

rejectedObservations.observation: Address format divergence (V3 uses lowercase, V2 uses checksummed) from v3-inverted-index review
rejectedObservations.reason: Functionally correct since lowercase is applied consistently in both storage and lookup. The mission AGENTS.md note is aspirational; changing it now would require updating both the index build and all callers. Not actionable as a library/AGENTS.md update — it's an implementation choice local to v3.py.

rejectedObservations.observation: UniswapRouterV2 singleton behavior might confuse future readers (from cross-area-regression-tests)
rejectedObservations.reason: Singleton pattern is already documented in y/classes/singleton.py. Adding AGENTS.md guidance on this would be overly specific. Reject as a convention note.

rejectedObservations.observation: mypy crashes on macOS when running on scripts/tests together with y/ (typed_envs plugin conflict)
rejectedObservations.reason: Already observed in v2-pool-index milestone. The services.yaml typecheck command only targets y/ (not scripts/ or tests/), so this is a non-issue for the defined validator. Existing guidance is sufficient.
